article.title: An Interview with Eric Seufert About Models and Ads, and AI’s Upside for Humanity

harvestedTerms.summary: A paywalled Stratechery interview with Eric Seufert covering generative AI model-building, Meta's foundational models, and advertising economics as a lens for AI optimism. The article excerpt is almost entirely a subscription prompt — no substantive content is extractable for terminology analysis.

article.title: Take our I/O 2026 quiz, vibe coded in Google AI Studio.

harvestedTerms.summary: A brief Google blog post promoting an I/O 2026 quiz built using vibe coding in Google AI Studio, powered by the Antigravity coding agent. The piece is light on substance — primarily a product showcase/marketing piece demonstrating that a non-developer editor built a quiz using Gemini and Google AI Studio.

article.title: Did Google’s AI agents really build an operating system for $916?

harvestedTerms.summary: Kapoor, Narayanan et al. (AI Snake Oil) critique Google's Antigravity 2.0 / Gemini 3.5 Flash 'OS-building agent' demo, focusing on methodological gaps: the 'single prompt' was thousands of lines, no dry-run reporting, no similarity analysis, no logs released. They introduce 'open-world evaluations' as a named paradigm and call for independent evaluation norms for long-horizon agentic tasks.

mustReadFlagged.reason: Kapoor, Narayanan et al. do something rare here: they take a headline AI demo seriously enough to dissect its methodology rather than dismiss or celebrate it. In doing so, they introduce 'open-world evaluation' as a named paradigm with its own methodological requirements — a genuine conceptual contribution that belongs in any serious vocabulary of AI assessment. Essential reading for anyone thinking about how to evaluate long-horizon agentic claims.

harvestedTerms.summary: Simon Willison's brief commentary on Anthropic's $65B Series H fundraise and the 'run-rate revenue' figure ($47B annualized), with a chart of Anthropic's revenue growth and a note on investor trust. Short news item with minimal conceptual depth.

article.title: Anthropic raises $65B in Series H funding at $965B post-money valuation

harvestedTerms.summary: Anthropic announces a $65B Series H funding round at a $965B valuation, citing growing enterprise adoption of Claude, $47B run-rate revenue, and major compute expansion deals with Amazon, Google, Broadcom, and SpaceX. The round includes strategic partnerships with memory/chip manufacturers Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix.
